verdict.note: Browser YATU (headless Chromium, disposable clone backend :8795 → vite :5183). #/issues Evals tab = 11 fresh eval rows; Threads tab = 13 issues, ZERO whose concern begins 'eval: ' (the 3 eval-remark threads on disk are absent — mergedIssues excludes them via isEvalConcern). The SAME eval thread absent from Threads IS present in the eval detail (entry.thread overlay). /api/issues confirmed 25 issues, 0 eval-concern leak.

Browser YATU: selected the evals-feed reading → EventDetail (.an-detail) renders media + REVIEW TRACK + composer. Typed a comment, Send → exactly ONE POST /api/remarks with body {node,scenario,body,codeSha} and NO author field (server-derived 'human'); after board reload the new remark renders as 'fv-reply remark open' with '● open' badge carrying the text. Session-tab data path proven directly: evalTimeline('evals-feed') readings carry .thread (remarkCount 1, openRemarks 1) — buildSessionEvals maps {...reading,inSession} so the session tab's EventDetail gets the identical thread; SessionEval passes specs/sessions/onWrite, guard removed, build compiles. No live session existed in the clone to pixel-drive the session tab (writing a session record would hit the shared ~/.spexcode runtime store).
